method: three-model design proposals (Opus 4.8, Gemini 2.5 Pro, GPT-5), final arbitration by Fable
fable_rationale: Twenty categories. Eight survived unanimously across all three models — that's the trustworthy core (Medicine, Biology, Computer Science & AI, Physics & Astronomy, Neuroscience, Public Health, Economics & Finance, Social Sciences). On the contested splits, I sided with the answer that maximizes 'feels impressively broad' without breaking MECE: I kept Chemistry separate from Materials Science (different journals, different professional identity, both individually large), kept Mathematics separate from Statistics (the most-cited disagreement in the diff and the one where pure mathematicians would walk if combined), and split Engineering into three children (EE/Robotics, Mech/Aero, Chemical/Bio) because Gemini AND GPT-5 both did so and the combined volume justifies it. I demoted Psychology under Neuroscience/Cognitive Sciences (GPT-5 was right to merge them — Brain & Cognitive is the modern frame and clinicians/psychologists self-identify with it), and I demoted my own Linguistics and Interdisciplinary entries since neither cleared a 2-of-3 vote — both fold cleanly into adjacent categories. I added Agriculture & Food (2-of-3) and Earth & Environmental (2-of-3), and ADDED Ecology & Evolution as a category even though only GPT-5 named it explicitly — every major university has a department, it's where ~3% of papers actually land, and lumping it into Biology hides its identity. Net: 20 categories, feels broad on the picker, every harvested paper finds a home, and the names match how a researcher would introduce themselves at a conference.

categories.name: Medicine & Clinical Science
categories.tagline: Diagnosis, surgery, therapy — bedside to bench.
covers: clinical specialties (cardiology, oncology, dermatology, neurology, surgery, pediatrics, etc.)
clinical trials, RCTs
NEJM, Lancet, JAMA, BMJ, specialty journals
pathology, radiology, anesthesia

categories.name: Public Health & Epidemiology
covers: epidemiology, biostatistics, health policy, global health, environmental health
Cochrane reviews
WHO data
outbreak modeling

categories.name: Biology
covers: molecular biology, cell biology, microbiology, genetics, developmental biology
Cell, Nature/Science life-science papers
model-organism work

categories.name: Ecology & Evolution
covers: population ecology, community ecology, evolutionary biology, biodiversity, paleontology, behavioral ecology

categories.name: Brain & Cognitive Sciences
covers: systems neuro, computational neuro, cognitive neuro, cognitive psychology, clinical and developmental psychology
neurology and psychiatry as cognitive disciplines

categories.name: Chemistry
covers: organic, inorganic, physical, analytical, electrochemistry
JACS, Angewandte, Chem Sci
explicitly_excludes: solid-state and electronic materials → Materials Science

categories.name: Materials Science
categories.tagline: Functional materials — solid-state to soft matter.
covers: semiconductors, polymers, ceramics, composites, nanomaterials, 2D materials, soft matter, biomaterials
Nature Materials, Adv Mater

categories.name: Physics & Astronomy
covers: high-energy, condensed matter, AMO, astrophysics, cosmology, geophysics-as-physics
Phys Rev family, arXiv hep/cond-mat/astro-ph

categories.name: Earth & Environmental Sciences
covers: climate science, atmospheric, oceanography, hydrology, geology, environmental chemistry, sustainability science

categories.name: Mathematics
covers: pure math (algebra, analysis, geometry, topology, number theory, logic, combinatorics)
applied math: dynamical systems, math-bio, math-physics
arXiv math.*

categories.name: Statistics & Data Science
covers: theoretical statistics, biostatistics, causal inference, MCMC, experimental design
JASA, Annals of Stats, Biometrika
data-science methods

categories.name: Computer Science & AI
covers: AI/ML, NLP, computer vision, systems, theory of computation, HCI, security, robotics-CS
arXiv cs.*, NeurIPS, ICML, CVPR, ACL

categories.name: Electrical & Electronic Engineering
covers: circuits, signals, communications, control theory, robotics-EE, photonics, semiconductors-as-devices, power systems

categories.name: Mechanical & Aerospace Engineering
covers: mechanical, aerospace, civil structural, manufacturing, mechanics of solids and fluids, propulsion
automotive, robotics-ME

categories.name: Chemical & Bioengineering
covers: chemical engineering, process design, biotechnology, biomedical engineering, tissue engineering, drug delivery

categories.name: Agriculture & Food Science
covers: agronomy, plant pathology, animal science, food science, nutrition science, aquaculture
AGRIS-style content

categories.name: Economics & Finance
covers: micro, macro, econometrics, behavioral economics, finance, accounting
AER, QJE, JFE, RFS, arXiv q-fin
explicitly_excludes: policy analysis without economics → Social Sciences

categories.name: Business & Management
covers: management science, organizational behavior, marketing research, operations research, entrepreneurship
AMJ, Strategic Management Journal

categories.name: Social Sciences
covers: sociology, anthropology, political science, education research, communication, criminology, geography-human, linguistics, science-and-technology-studies

categories.name: Humanities
covers: history of science, philosophy (including phil-of-science), archaeology, classics, art history, literary studies that engage empirical methods

majority_2of3_kept: Mathematics (Opus + GPT-5; rejected the math+stats merge)
Statistics & Data Science (Opus + GPT-5; rejected the math+stats merge)
Electrical Engineering (Gemini + GPT-5; split from Engineering)
fable_overrides: ADDED Ecology & Evolution (1/3 — only GPT-5). University departments exist; ~3% of papers; lumping into Biology hides identity.
ADDED Business & Management (1/3 — only GPT-5). Distinct journals, distinct audience; lumping into Economics misleads users.
MERGED Opus's Psychology + Neuroscience → Brain & Cognitive Sciences (per GPT-5). Modern, MECE, fewer cards.
DROPPED Opus's Linguistics (1/3). Folds cleanly into Social Sciences.
DROPPED Opus's Interdisciplinary (1/3). Catches papers that should be assigned a home, not exiled.
design_decisions.audience_pct_totals: Sum: 105% — slight overcount because of dual-belonging papers (e.g. neuro-immunology counts in both Brain and Medicine when surfaced). Per-paper assignment will still be 1-of-20 via classifier confidence.

uncovered_concerns: Veterinary medicine: still no card. ~0.5% of papers. Acceptable to fold under Medicine or Agriculture per paper.
Architecture & Design: still no card. Empirical papers fit under relevant engineering or social-science categories.

Dermatology, cardiology, neurology, oncology etc.: NOT level-1 categories. These are level-2 children of Medicine — see the deep taxonomy. The picker shows Medicine; the personalized feed shows the user's subspecialty when they drill in.

AI4Science, complex systems, network science: these self-label as their home discipline (CS, Physics, Biology) at the level-1 picker; the deep taxonomy still tags them as interdisciplinary at lower levels.
